result:
Based on my research, I need to clarify an important finding: In Slay the Spire 2, "Corpse Slug" appears as a **regular monster encounter** in the Underdocks (Act 1b), not as a boss. The search results show that 
Underdocks Monsters include Corpse Slugs
, while 
Underdocks Bosses are Lagavulin Matriarch, Soul Fysh, and Waterfall Giant
. That said, Corpse Slugs can appear in groups and have notable mechanics. Here is the JSON based on available data:

```json
{
  "boss": "Corpse Slug",
  "character": "The Silent",
  "note": "Corpse Slug is a monster encounter in Act 1b (Underdocks), not a boss. Multiple Corpse Slugs appear together, and the fight can be quite threatening due to their scaling. The following advice treats this as a dangerous encounter rather than a formal boss fight.",
  "boss_mechanics": "Corpse Slugs cycle through Whip Slap -> Glomp -> Gloop in a repeating pattern. The first Corpse Slug picks its opening move randomly, and each subsequent slug starts on the next move in the cycle. Critically, Corpse Slugs have the 'Ravenous' passive: when an ally dies, a surviving Corpse Slug gains 4 Strength (5 at higher Ascension). This means killing them one at a time causes the survivors to ramp up dangerously. Gloop likely applies Slimed status cards that clog your deck.",
  "strategy": "Because of the Ravenous passive, try to spread your damage evenly across all Corpse Slugs and kill them on the same turn or in quick succession to minimize Strength snowballing. The Silent's Poison is ideal here since it ticks on all poisoned enemies simultaneously at the start of their turns, bypassing the need to focus fire. Use Noxious Fumes or Bouncing Flask to apply Poison to all slugs at once. Prioritize blocking on Glomp turns, which tend to deal the most damage, and use the Gloop turn to set up your card engine. If you must kill them sequentially, save your strongest block cards for after a slug dies, since the survivors will hit much harder with their Strength boost.",
  "priority_cards": [
    "Noxious Fumes",
    "Bouncing Flask",
    "Deadly Poison",
    "Blade Dance",
    "Cloak and Dagger",
    "Catalyst",
    "Piercing Wail",
    "Acrobatics",
    "Haze"
  ],
  "cards_to_avoid_playing": [
    "Expensive single-target attacks with no scaling",
    "Slow setup powers if slugs are already buffed with Strength",
    "Any card that generates Slimed or adds bloat to your deck when Gloop is about to fire"
  ],
  "hp_threshold": "55+ HP recommended. While not a boss, multiple Corpse Slugs with Ravenous Strength scaling can deal surprising burst damage if one dies early. The Underdocks demands solid defense against bursty attacks.",
  "potion_advice": "Use Poison Potions or Weak Potions early to slow down the slugs' output and apply passive damage. If a slug dies and survivors gain Strength, immediately use a defensive potion (Block Potion) or a Shackling Potion to negate the Strength spike. Save explosive potions like Attack Potions for a turn where you can finish off multiple slugs simultaneously."
}
```
